title: Oscars 2026: Best Picture Nominees Ranked
text: IndieWire's ranking from worst to best: Bugonia is a Yorgos Lanthimos film about a galaxy-brained conspiracist beekeeper who kidnaps a pharma executive. F1 is Joseph Kosinski's racing blockbuster that earns nominations below the line but lacks the impact of comparable films. Frankenstein is Del Toro's Netflix adaptation of Shelley's classic that impresses with practical effects but feels reverential rather than daring. Sentimental Value is Joachim Trier's family drama reuniting him with actress Renate Reinsve. Marty Supreme stars Timothée Chalamet as an unlikable protagonist in this character study. Hamnet is Chloé Zhao's unspeakably devastating meditation on loss and creativity. Train Dreams is Clint Bentley's meditation on existence through one man's perspective. Sinners is Ryan Coogler's supernatural thriller featuring Michael B. Jordan in dual roles as conflicted criminals, a daring studio movie that blends genre thrills with serious dramatic weight. One Battle After Another is Paul Thomas Anderson's propulsive, hilarious, and overwhelmingly tender paranoid thriller that accurately crystallizes how fucking anxious it feels to be alive right now through ambitious storytelling. The Secret Agent is Kleber Mendonça Filho's Brazilian film showcasing Wagner Moura's powerful performance as a professor in 1977 fascist Brazil.
